title: virola multiflora
intro: Virola multiflora, also known as the Guinaldo tree, is a species of plant native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain a rich source of psychoactive compounds, including bufotenin and DMT. The medicinal properties of Virola multiflora are used to treat a range of health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, and it is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Virola multiflora are typically made by boiling the roots in water, and can be consumed as a tea or used as a topical application to promote healing and relaxation.
